commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Anthropic Claude CLI backend and generic/live CLI runner paths so resumed Claude CLI sessions keep harness system-prompt args, adds regression tests, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +126, Docs +1. Total +156 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main combines `systemPromptWhen: "first"`, `!useResume` prompt gates, and live-session stripping, so resumed Claude CLI paths drop the harness prompt. I did not run a billed live Claude resume, but the linked issue and PR proof give a concrete argv-level reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair worker can remove the release-owned changelog entry without changing the runtime fix; maintainer still owns the final compatibility/live-smoke decision.

target.title: fix(anthropic-cli): re-inject system prompt on claude --resume (#80374)
target.body:
## Summary

Resumed `claude --resume` turns lost the harness system prompt (SOUL.md / IDENTITY.md / USER.md / etc.), so resumed sessions behaved as generic Claude until the user manually re-loaded. Three things were happening:

1. The `claude-cli` backend defaulted `systemPromptWhen: "first"`, which silently dropped the prompt from `resolveSystemPromptUsage` for resumed sessions.
2. Even with `systemPromptWhen: "always"`, the generic CLI runner gated tempfile creation in `executePreparedCliRun` on `!useResume`, so the system-prompt tempfile that the upstream resolver had decided to propagate was never written.
3. Even when the tempfile was present, `buildCliArgs` and `buildClaudeLiveArgs` both stripped the prompt args on resume, undoing the work at the arg-builder stage and the live-stdio strip stage respectively.

This PR addresses all four affected paths (per @TomTheHand's review in #81199):

- `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` — default `systemPromptWhen: "always"` for `claude-cli`.
- `src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts` — write the system-prompt tempfile whenever `resolveSystemPromptUsage` decided a prompt should be propagated, including on resume.
- `src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts` (`buildCliArgs`) — emit `--append-system-prompt-file`, the Codex `model_instructions_file` config-override, and the inline `--append-system-prompt` on resume when `backend.systemPromptWhen === "always"`. Backends staying on `"first"` keep the previous behavior because `resolveSystemPromptUsage` returns `null` for their resumes upstream.
- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` (`buildClaudeLiveArgs` / `stripLiveProcessArgs`) — only strip `systemPromptArg` / `systemPromptFileArg` from live-stdio resume argv when `backend.systemPromptWhen !== "always"`. The `--session-id` strip continues unchanged (live sessions correlate by Claude's own resume id, not the OpenClaw stub).

Fixes #80374. Supersedes the partial fix that previously closed unmerged in #80376 (which removed `!params.useResume` guards entirely); this PR uses the narrower `systemPromptWhen === "always"` gate so non-anthropic backends are unaffected.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.helpers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` — 140 passed (includes new regression coverage for the three runner paths).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` — 38 passed (covers `executePreparedCliRun` end-to-end).

New regression tests in this PR:
- `buildCliArgs > re-emits --append-system-prompt-file on resumed claude-cli sessions when systemPromptWhen='always' (#80374)`
- `buildCliArgs > still drops --append-system-prompt-file on resumed sessions for systemPromptWhen='first' backends`
- `buildCliArgs > re-emits Codex model_instructions_file on resume when systemPromptWhen='always'`
- `buildCliArgs > re-emits --append-system-prompt on resume when systemPromptWhen='always' and only the inline arg is configured`
- `cli-runner.spawn > preserves --append-system-prompt-file on resumed live sessions when systemPromptWhen='always' (#80374)`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Resumed `claude --resume` turns dropped `--append-system-prompt-file` / `--append-system-prompt` and therefore lost the harness system prompt. SOUL/IDENTITY/USER content only loaded on fresh sessions; resumed sessions behaved as generic Claude until `/reset` or `/reload`. Root cause is split across four code paths: backend default (`systemPromptWhen: "first"`), runner tempfile creation gated on `!useResume` in `executePreparedCliRun`, prompt-flag emission gated on `!useResume` in `buildCliArgs`, and prompt-flag stripping unconditional on `useResume` in `buildClaudeLiveArgs`/`stripLiveProcessArgs`. Reviewer @TomTheHand identified the latter three and verified the combined patch produces correct resumed live argv against compiled OpenClaw 2026.5.6.

Real environment tested: branch `fix-80374-claude-cli-resume-system-prompt` at HEAD `5a4f6236a8` checked out as a worktree of openclaw/openclaw, with node_modules shared from the main checkout. Drove the on-branch `buildCliArgs` and a reproduction of the `stripLiveProcessArgs` gate directly through `node`/`tsx` to capture the resumed-argv emission on both `systemPromptWhen: "always"` and `systemPromptWhen: "first"` backends.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
$ node node_modules/.bin/tsx demo-80374.mjs
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.helpers.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts
```

The demo imports the on-branch `buildCliArgs` and inlines the `stripLiveProcessArgs` gate verbatim from `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts`, then runs both branches (`always` / `first`) against an `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts`-shaped backend with a representative resume argv.

Evidence after fix: live `node`/`tsx` stdout against the on-branch source (HEAD `5a4f6236a8`):
```
=== Backend systemPromptWhen='always' (claude-cli post-fix default) ===
Resume args from buildCliArgs:
[
  "-p",
  "--resume",
  "abc123",
  "--append-system-prompt-file",
  "/tmp/openclaw/system-prompt.md"
]

=== Backend systemPromptWhen='first' (legacy/other backends) ===
Resume args from buildCliArgs:
[
  "-p",
  "--resume",
  "abc123"
]

=== buildClaudeLiveArgs strip-gate simulation, systemPromptWhen='always' ===
[
  "-p",
  "--resume",
  "abc123",
  "--append-system-prompt-file",
  "/tmp/openclaw/system-prompt.md"
]

=== buildClaudeLiveArgs strip-gate simulation, systemPromptWhen='first' ===
[
  "-p",
  "--resume",
  "abc123"
]
```

Observed result after fix: A resumed `claude --resume` turn on the `claude-cli` backend now keeps `--append-system-prompt-file <tmp>/system-prompt.md` on argv all the way through to the spawned process — both for the generic CLI runner path and for the `claude-stdio` live-session path. The harness system prompt re-injects on every resumed turn, so SOUL/IDENTITY/USER content keeps shaping the agent across `/resume` boundaries. Backends that stay on `systemPromptWhen: "first"` (the default for non-anthropic CLI backends, e.g. codex, gemini) preserve their previous behavior — resumed argv still drops the prompt flags — because `resolveSystemPromptUsage` returns `null` for those resumes upstream and the new guard is a strict widening of the previous condition.

What was not tested: live `claude --resume` against a real Anthropic-billed session in this environment. The four-path fix is exact (each guard is a narrow widening conditional on `backend.systemPromptWhen === "always"`), the regression suite covers each path, and @TomTheHand verified the combined patch against compiled OpenClaw 2026.5.6 in the linked review comment. A maintainer-side live `claude --resume` round trip with a SOUL.md identity is the natural confirming step.

Closes #80374.
target.author: lonexreb
